About: It is about the Virgin Mary as the protector and patroness of the city of Kazan.
It is about the Virgin Mary as a palladium of all of Russia and Rus'.
It is about the Virgin Mary as the Holy Protectress of Russia.
Affects: It affects the Russian peasantry's perception of the Revolution of 1905.
It affects the Russian peasantry's perception of Russia's defeat in the Russo-Japanese War of 1904–1905.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Russian Orthodox Church.
It is associated with the Eastern Orthodox Church.
It is associated with the city of Kazan.
It is associated with Russia.
It is associated with Rus'.
It is associated with Constantinople.
It is associated with Moscow.

It is associated with Fátima, Portugal.
It is associated with the Vatican.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the greater Eastern Orthodox Church.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a gold frame ornamented with many jewels.
CreatedAround: It was created around the 16th century.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 16th century.
CreatedTo: It was created to protect and patronize the city of Kazan.

It was created to serve as the Holy Protectress of Russia.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by a 10-year-old girl named Matrona.

HasA: It has a gold frame ornamented with many jewels.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being a holy icon of the highest stature within the Russian Orthodox Church.
It is characterized by being venerated by all Orthodox faithful.
It is characterized by its association with the Virgin Mary.
It is characterized by its role as protector and patroness of the city of Kazan.
It is characterized by its role as a palladium of all of Russia and Rus'.
It is characterized by its nickname, the Holy Protectress of Russia.
It is characterized by its legend of original acquisition from Constantinople.
It is characterized by its legend of being lost in 1438.
It is characterized by its legend of miraculous recovery in 1579.
It is characterized by its copies displayed in numerous churches.
HeldIn: It was held in the Kazan Convent of the Theotokos.
It was held in St. Petersburg.
It was housed in Fátima, Portugal from 1970 to 1993.
It was housed in the study of Pope John Paul II in the Vatican from 1993 to 2004.
It was kept in the Kazan Monastery of the Theotokos.
It was kept in the Annunciation Cathedral of the Kazan Kremlin.
It is now enshrined in the Cathedral of the Elevation of the Holy Cross, part of the Convent of the Theotokos.
Influenced: It influenced the belief that the disappearance of the icon was a sign of tragedies to plague Russia.
It influenced the Russian peasantry's belief that miseries of the Revolution of 1905 were due to its desecration.
It influenced the Russian peasantry's belief that Russia's defeat in the Russo-Japanese War was due to its desecration.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Virgin Mary's appearance in a dream.

It was influenced by the Siege of Leningrad during World War II.

About: それはカザン市の保護者であり後援者である聖母マリアについてのものです。

それはロシアの農民が1904年から1905年の露日戦争でのロシアの敗北をどのように認識するかに影響を与えます。
